theme: Shortest program that prints the given string
title: Multi-Scale Grammar Compression
strategies: literal baseline: one rule listing every character as a terminal (no reuse at all)

LZW-style greedy dictionary: single left-to-right pass, extend the current phrase by one character while the extended phrase is already a known dictionary entry, otherwise emit a reference for the current phrase and register a new +1-character dictionary entry

RePair-style bottom-up digram merging: repeatedly find the most frequent adjacent symbol pair across the WHOLE current sequence (of terminals and already-built rules) and factor it into one new reusable rule, iterating until no pair repeats -- rediscovers atoms, then blocks of atoms, then sections of blocks, bottom-up
hybrid: run bottom-up digram merging to convergence, then re-scan the surviving top-level sequence for any remaining length-2 run to squeeze a little further
